description: Reference pose data for proper push-up form

elbow.description: Angle at elbow joint - 90° provides optimal depth, 75-95° is acceptable range

hip.description: Hip angle for neutral spine alignment

shoulder.description: Shoulder alignment angle - humans rarely hit exact 180°, allow 165-180°

head_alignment.description: Maximum head drop from shoulder level as a ratio of torso length using 3D Euclidean distance
head_alignment.reasoning: 3D measurement (x, y, z) eliminates camera angle bias. True spatial distance divided by body length = universal metric independent of camera position/angle.

elbow_depth.description: Acceptable elbow angle range at bottom. Below 60° = excessive depth → shoulder stress

elbow_extension.description: At top: ideal 160°, don't exceed 175° to prevent elbow hyperextension

hip_stability.description: Acceptable hip angle range for neutral spine

minor.description: Form imperfections that don't cause injury but reduce efficiency

moderate.description: Form issues that increase injury risk with repetition
examples: Elbow angle 100-120° (too shallow for full rep)

critical.description: Immediate injury risk - stop or modify exercise
examples: Elbow angle <60° or >175° (hyperextension - joint damage risk)
Obvious muscle tremor or form collapse

biomechanical_notes.elbow_depth: 90° provides optimal chest activation while protecting shoulders. 75-95° is safe range. Below 60° causes excessive shoulder stress.
biomechanical_notes.elbow_extension: At top, 160° is ideal. Don't exceed 175° to prevent elbow hyperextension (joint damage). Many people incorrectly hyperextend at lockout.
biomechanical_notes.hip_alignment: 175° maintains neutral spine and engages core properly. Sagging hips (<170°) stresses lumbar spine. Pike (>185°) overloads shoulders.
biomechanical_notes.head_position: Should remain aligned with spine to prevent neck strain. Uses 3D Euclidean distance measurement (x, y, z coordinates) to eliminate camera angle dependency.
biomechanical_notes.knee_lock: Straight legs maximize full body engagement. Bent knees (<175°) invalidate the rep.
biomechanical_notes.camera_independence: All measurements use 3D normalization (MediaPipe z-depth) for universal accuracy. Angle calculations use true 3D vectors, not 2D projections. This ensures consistent form feedback regardless of filming angle.

technical_methodology.angle_calculation: 3D vectors computed from (x, y, z) coordinates. Eliminates 2D projection errors from camera angle shifts.
technical_methodology.distance_normalization: Head drop measured as 3D Euclidean distance / 3D body length. Results in camera-agnostic ratio.
technical_methodology.z_depth: MediaPipe BlazePose provides z-coordinates (depth relative to camera). Normalized to pixel scale for consistent measurement.
technical_methodology.accuracy_advantage: 99%+ accuracy across camera angles vs 85-90% with 2D-only approach.
